paragraphs.body: Everyone in this world has a sphere around their life, meaning when that sphere is perfectly intact, life is well and calm. However, everyone’s spheres overlap at some point, which sets up for disaster. The Bermuda Triangle also has a sphere around it, and when the waters are calm and the skies are clear, it is seen to be heavenly, but when the storms arise and the sphere is broken, disaster strikes, again.

paragraphs.body: I recently read an article titled, “Bermuda Triangle Demystified?”, written by Aria Dismont Nathan, which dove right into all the mysteries of the Bermuda Triangle and all the assumptions circulating the dangers of the so called “Devil’s Island”. She gives plenty of historical background to support why there are so many suspicions. It debunks all the sorcery and witchcraft that people suspect with scientific reasons, however, she still claims that there still is something magical. The Bermuda Triangle has always fascinated me by it’s beautiful nature, but also all the mysteries and uncertainly of the missing planes and ships. I think what fascinates me the most is the idea of the unknown. I continue to ask myself, is it better to keep searching for answers and risk damaging our spheres, or is it best to not know anything at all?

annotations.text: The Bermuda Triangle has always fascinated me by it’s beautiful nature, but also all the mysteries and uncertainly of the missing planes and ships. I think what fascinates me the most is the idea of the unknown.
annotations.comment: Remember that a summary is all "they say," no "I say." Try using the summary templates/writing moves we've worked on in class to create a more complete summary that "points" to your own essay's ideas (the "art" of summary).

paragraphs.body: My sphere has been broken through plenty of times, and I constantly live with the fear of uncertainty. This is all due to my parents’ addiction. Both of my parents have battled with their own issues and struggles, but even when they may be “clean”, there is still a risk that it could happen again. Ever since I was a little girl, I would say around third grade, I was aware of addiction was. Both of them had past issues of struggling with substance abuse when they were teenagers, but when my parents met, they were in recovery. My parents had me young, so they were not fully equip to take on the responsibility of a child, but they made it work. As I was growing up, I lived with my mom and visited my dad on the weekends, they were very good at co-parenting. I was always able to have two of everything and I loved it as a little girl, and even though I had two of everything, my parents still would come to each other’s family holidays and allow for family time, so I never has any worries, and all of the spheres were aligned. However, my life was completely changed when the first disaster tore a tiny hole in my sphere.

paragraphs.body: When I was in halfway through my sixth-grade year, I was visiting my dad during the weekend, and we received a call from my grandmother telling us my mother had been arrested and we didn’t know when she would be released. I had no idea that this would be the beginning of spheres overlapping. I ended up moving in with my dad and transferring schools halfway through that year, little did I know, my dad was also using when I moved in with him and my other grandmother. Fast forwarding to eighth grade my mother had been clean, and I moved back in with her and my grandmother. After I had moved back in with them, another hole was ripped right through my sphere, this one leaving an even bigger destruction. My father’s battle with heroin had become extremely devastating and he had put me in danger so my mother thought it would be best to stop seeing him as much, and those visits became nothing, and the phone stopped ringing. He has been in and out of my life ever since it started back in eigth-grade and is still currently happening to this day.

annotations.text: My father’s battle with heroin had become extremely devastating and he had put me in danger so my mother thought it would be best to stop seeing him as much, and those visits became nothing, and the phone stopped ringing. He has been in and out of my life ever since it started back in eigth-grade and is still currently happening to this day.
annotations.comment: Wow. What a devastating story. Let's talk about this story and see what develops in terms of your audience and purpose for this essay. :)

paragraphs.body: The severity of my mother’s situation, on the other hand, begins more recently. When I was young, my mother was using throughout my childhood, but I was completely unaware of it because I was a child, and I was supposed to be protected from those kinds of issues. Ever since I had moved back in with my mom, we rebuilt our relationship and we became best friends, she was my best friend. I noticed a change between her and I around a year ago, I slowly started to distance myself away from her because of her actions, but we remained close. More recently we became more distant, and I noticed something was off but never suspected the worst, I just brushed it off. In July I had my graduation party, and I won’t go into detail, but it didn’t end so well. My mother decided that day that she would be moving out and never wanted to speak to me again or have me as her responsibility anymore. This by far completely broke my sphere apart. We came to later find out about a week before I moved into college, she had begun using drugs again and it wasn’t just alcohol. I was devastated hearing this news, and she still has no idea that I know to this day. At that point and still to this day I must ask myself what was real and what was just all just a lie.

paragraphs.body: You are probably wondering what this has to do with the Bermuda Triangle, well I’ll tell you. Every plane or ship has its routine route that it is on, however when there has been plenty of history of disappearing ships and plane crashes, there is a fear that it may happen again, because once it happens one time, it could happen again. At any given moment your sphere could come crashing down. Everyone has their daily routines and they people they love, but when those people go off of their route, it leads to disasters, overlapping their spears with who’s their affecting. When dealing with addiction it is all out of everyone’s control, and there is always uncertainty of it happening again. In the article, Bermuda Triangle Demystified, author Aria Dismount Nathan acknowledges the disappearances of ships and aircrafts, leaving us with a worrisome feeling. Robinson informs, “In the past 500 years at least 50 ships and 20 aircraft have vanished in the Triangle, most without a trace -- no wreckage, no bodies, no nothing. Many disappeared in reportedly calm waters, without having sent a distress signal.” This quote signifies the idea that even when all is calm, disaster can still happen. There will always be an uncertainly because if it happens once, it can always happen again. Disaster can happen at any given moment and there may be no signs of it coming. You never know what may be destroyed within a matter of seconds. Addiction is a disease that not only affects the addict, but it drowns everyone that loves them as well. Robinson indicates, “But perhaps the most damning tales were told by sailors terrified of shipwreck on Bermuda's treacherous stretch of reefs.” This quote is basically trying to say that all the myths originated from those who have experienced such horrors of the Triangle and have had to be cautious of getting caught up in the storm. This relates back to addiction because the only people that know the true outcomes of what happens with addicts are the people that have experienced to firsthand and watched it happen. They themselves are scared that one day that will be them or one day they are never going to live a normal life.

paragraphs.body: I am here to tell you that no matter what disasters happen when your sphere is disrupted, and you feel like it will never get better, there will always be a beauty to it all. The Bermuda Triangle will always have assumptions about it, but no one can deny how beautiful it is beyond all the rumors. The people in my life now have helped me rebuild my sphere and grow as a person still to this day. So yes, there are explanations to everything, and reality is true, but there is something special that will always be there. You must have faith that everything will fall into to place, and the spheres will align, and you can’t worry about the things out of you’re control. Works Cited Nathan, Aria, Dismont. “Bermuda Triangle Demystified?” New York: The New York Times, https://archive.nytimes.com/www.nytimes.com/fodors/top/features/travel/destinations/bermudaa ndcaribbean/bermuda/fdrs_feat_29_8.html?n=Top%2FFeatures%2FTravel%2FDestinations%2F Bermuda+and+Caribbean%2FBermuda
